title: Ayushman Bharat Claim Submission SOP
summary: Defines the end-to-end Ayushman Bharat claim submission flow, including required documents, verification checks, claim packet preparation, and final submission controls.
content:
## Purpose
This SOP defines the standard Ayushman Bharat or PM-JAY claim submission workflow followed by the insurance desk, admission desk, and billing team.

## Required Documents
- Ayushman card copy
- Patient government ID proof
- Admission record with UHID and date
- Diagnosis or procedure notes from the treating doctor
- Investigation reports supporting admission
- Discharge summary signed by the consultant
- Supporting hospital forms and signed claim form

## Claim Initiation Workflow
- Verify Ayushman card details and match the patient identity at admission.
- Confirm the package eligibility and clinical indication for the planned treatment.
- Record admission details and create the scheme-specific claim file.
- Collect mandatory clinical, diagnostic, and billing documents before discharge closure.
- Submit the final claim packet only after all evidence documents are signed and complete.

## Validation Checks Before Submission
- Ensure the package code matches the diagnosis and procedure notes.
- Ensure the discharge summary is complete, dated, and signed.
- Ensure the identity proof and Ayushman card copy are legible.
- Ensure supporting hospital forms are attached to the packet.

## Source Guidance
This document is used by the insurance desk when staff ask what documents are required for Ayushman claim submission.

title: Ayushman Planned Admission and Pre-Authorization Guideline
summary: Explains when pre-authorization is required for Ayushman cases, what information must be checked before planned admission, and how the approval workflow is handled.
content:
## Purpose
This guideline defines pre-authorization and planned admission checks for Ayushman Bharat cases.

## Pre-Authorization Rules
- Pre-authorization is required for planned admissions and for procedures where the package guidance asks for insurer approval before treatment.
- Emergency stabilization can begin first, but the insurance desk must complete the approval workflow as soon as the patient is clinically stable.
- No planned procedure should be scheduled until the approval request packet is complete.

## Planned Admission Workflow
- Verify Ayushman eligibility and beneficiary identity.
- Confirm whether the selected package requires pre-authorization.
- Prepare clinical summary, diagnosis details, estimated package, and supporting investigations.
- Send the pre-authorization request to the approving desk or payer workflow.
- Track approval timeline and record the reference number in the patient file.

## Required Approval Inputs
- Beneficiary identity details
- Diagnosis summary
- Package requested
- Doctor recommendation
- Relevant diagnostic evidence

## Source Guidance
This document answers questions about whether pre-authorization is required before planned admission.

title: Ayushman Claim Rejection and Resubmission SOP
summary: Lists common Ayushman claim rejection reasons, mandatory validation checks, and the resubmission workflow used by the insurance desk.
content:
## Purpose
This SOP is used when staff ask why Ayushman claims get rejected and how they should be corrected for resubmission.

## Common Rejection Reasons
- Missing patient ID proof or unreadable card copy
- Incorrect package code selection
- Incomplete or unsigned discharge summary
- Missing diagnosis or procedure notes
- Late submission after the allowed window
- Missing supporting hospital forms

## Resubmission Workflow
- Review the rejection remark and classify the issue as document, coding, or timeline related.
- Reopen the claim checklist and identify which evidence is missing.
- Obtain corrected discharge summary, coding, or physician documents.
- Revalidate the full claim packet before resubmission.
- Resubmit only after the insurance desk supervisor signs off the correction set.

## Prevention Checklist
- Use the latest SOP version before final submission.
- Cross-check package code against diagnosis and procedure notes.
- Confirm signatures on all clinical summary documents.
- Confirm the claim packet is submitted within the allowed timeline.

## Source Guidance
This document supports answers about rejection reasons and missing document warnings.

title: Hospital Discharge and Billing Workflow
summary: Explains the discharge and billing flow from doctor clearance to patient handover, including final insurance checks and discharge summary controls.
content:
## Purpose
This SOP explains the discharge workflow and final billing controls for insured and Ayushman patients.

## Discharge Workflow
- Treating doctor records discharge clearance.
- Nursing team completes the discharge checklist and counselling notes.
- Pharmacy and diagnostics close all open items.
- Billing validates dues, package notes, and insurer references.
- Insurance desk verifies that discharge documents are ready for final claim use.
- Patient receives the discharge packet and counselling.

## Required Discharge Documents
- Signed discharge summary
- Final bill or billing note
- Medication reconciliation sheet
- Insurance approval reference if applicable

## Comparison Note
Compared with the claim SOP, this document focuses on operational closure, patient handover, and billing completion rather than eligibility and submission rules.

title: Package Eligibility and Clinical Documentation Checklist
summary: Provides the clinical documentation and package validation checklist used before Ayushman submission and during payer review.
content:
## Purpose
This checklist supports package validation and clinical documentation quality for Ayushman cases.

## Clinical Documentation Checklist
- Diagnosis summary must match the package requested.
- Procedure notes must support the selected code.
- Investigation reports must justify the admission or intervention.
- Consultant notes must be legible and signed.
- Discharge summary must reflect the final diagnosis and procedure performed.

## Staff Validation Steps
- Compare package code with diagnosis and procedure notes.
- Check the admission record, investigations, and consultant notes together.
- Confirm the final summary reflects the complete treatment timeline.

## Source Guidance
This checklist is commonly cited when users ask what supporting clinical documents are needed.

title: Insurance Desk Hindi Response Guide
summary: Provides simplified Hindi phrasing for explaining Ayushman claim steps, required documents, and admission guidance to staff and patients.
content:
## उद्देश्य
यह गाइड इंश्योरेंस डेस्क को आयुष्मान प्रक्रिया हिंदी में समझाने के लिए बनाई गई है।

## जरूरी दस्तावेज
- आयुष्मान कार्ड की कॉपी
- मरीज की पहचान का प्रमाण
- भर्ती रिकॉर्ड
- डॉक्टर के निदान या प्रक्रिया नोट्स
- डिस्चार्ज समरी
- अस्पताल के सहायक फॉर्म

## सरल कार्यप्रवाह
- पहले कार्ड और पहचान की जांच करें।
- फिर पैकेज और प्री-ऑथ की जरूरत देखें।
- सभी क्लिनिकल और बिलिंग दस्तावेज इकट्ठा करें।
- सब दस्तावेज पूरे होने के बाद ही क्लेम सबमिट करें।

## उपयोग
यह गाइड उन सवालों के लिए उपयोगी है जिनमें हिंदी में प्रक्रिया समझानी हो।

title: Ayushman Bharat Health Coverage Guidelines
summary: Sample Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY policy document covering eligibility, coverage limits, covered services, exclusions, claim process, and required documents.
content:
## Policy Metadata
Policy Title: Ayushman Bharat Health Coverage Guidelines
Policy ID: PMJAY-HOSP-001
Effective Date: 01-Jan-2026

## Objective
To provide cashless healthcare services to eligible beneficiaries under the Ayushman Bharat scheme across empaneled hospitals.

## Eligibility Criteria
- Families listed in SECC database
- Valid Ayushman Card (PM-JAY e-card)
- Aadhaar or any government-approved ID

## Coverage Details
- Coverage up to 5,00,000 per family per year
- Pre-hospitalization for 3 days
- Post-hospitalization for 15 days
- Diagnostics and medicines

## Services Covered
- General Surgery
- Cardiology procedures
- Orthopedics
- Maternity services
- Oncology under limited packages

## Exclusions
- Cosmetic procedures
- OPD consultations unless part of package
- Experimental treatments

## Claim Process
- Patient verification
- e-KYC validation
- Pre-authorization
- Treatment approval
- Discharge upload
- Settlement within 15 to 30 days

## Required Documents
- Ayushman Card
- ID proof
- Doctor prescription
- Discharge summary

## Source Guidance
This document should be used for Ayushman card eligibility, coverage, exclusions, and required-document questions.

title: Patient Admission and Registration Policy
summary: Hospital admission policy covering admission types, onboarding workflow, required documents, emergency admission handling, and role responsibilities.
content:
## Policy Metadata
Policy Title: Patient Admission and Registration Policy
Policy ID: HOSP-ADM-101
Department: Front Desk / Admission

## Purpose
To standardize patient admission procedures and ensure smooth onboarding.

## Admission Types
- Emergency Admission
- Planned Admission
- Daycare Admission

## Admission Procedure
- Registration
- Capture patient details
- Insurance verification
- Bed allocation
- Consent signing

## Required Documents
- ID proof
- Insurance or Ayushman card
- Referral letter where applicable

## Emergency Admission
Immediate treatment must begin without delay and documentation can be completed after stabilization.

## Responsibilities
- Front Desk: Registration
- Doctor: Evaluation
- Billing: Insurance validation

## Source Guidance
This document is useful for admission workflow, required documents at the front desk, and emergency admission questions.

title: In-Hospital Insurance Claim Workflow

content:

title: Hospital Bed Allocation and Ward Transfer SOP
summary: Dummy hospital operations policy for bed allocation, ward transfer approval, isolation routing, and occupancy documentation.
content:
## Purpose
To ensure safe and traceable bed allocation across emergency, planned admission, and post-operative ward transfers.

## Bed Allocation Workflow
- Confirm admission category and clinical priority.
- Check ward occupancy and isolation requirement.
- Assign bed category according to treatment plan and payer eligibility.
- Record transfer reason and approving doctor for any ward movement.

## Required Records
- Admission registration number
- Bed allocation note
- Transfer approval note if ward movement occurs
- Nursing handover entry

## Source Guidance
This dummy hospital policy supports general hospital operations questions unrelated to Ayushman claims.

title: Laboratory and Diagnostic Turnaround Policy
summary: Dummy hospital diagnostic policy covering sample collection windows, routine turnaround times, urgent processing, and escalation rules.
content:
## Purpose
To define turnaround targets for routine, urgent, and critical diagnostic services in the hospital.

## Routine Targets
- Basic biochemistry within 4 hours
- CBC within 2 hours
- Routine X-ray within 3 hours

## Urgent Processing Rules
- Mark emergency samples at the time of collection.
- Escalate critical-value tests directly to the duty doctor.
- Record delay reasons in the laboratory incident log.

## Source Guidance
This dummy policy helps answer general hospital questions about diagnostics and service timelines.

title: ICU Escalation and Critical Care Handover SOP
summary: Dummy ICU policy for escalation triggers, mandatory clinical handover fields, shift ownership, and audit traceability.
content:
## Purpose
To standardize escalation and handover practices for critically ill patients in ICU and step-down units.

## Escalation Triggers
- Persistent hypotension despite first-line treatment
- New respiratory distress requiring ventilator support
- Sudden fall in Glasgow Coma Scale score
- Critical lab alerts requiring consultant review

## Handover Checklist
- Active diagnosis and treatment plan
- Current hemodynamic and respiratory status
- Pending investigations and expected review time
- High-risk medications and infusion details
- Family communication note and consent status

## Governance
- Duty intensivist validates every shift handover.
- Delay above 15 minutes must be recorded in the ICU variance log.

## Source Guidance
This dummy SOP supports queries on ICU handover quality, escalation conditions, and critical-care documentation.

title: Biomedical Waste Segregation and Infection Control Policy
summary: Dummy infection-control policy on biomedical waste segregation, color-coded bins, PPE compliance, and breach escalation.
content:
## Purpose
To enforce safe biomedical waste segregation and reduce healthcare-associated infections across all care areas.

## Segregation Rules
- Yellow bin: pathological and infectious waste.
- Red bin: contaminated recyclable items after disinfection protocol.
- White puncture-proof container: sharps and needles.
- Blue container: glassware and metallic implants.

## PPE and Handling Controls
- Gloves and mask are mandatory during waste handling.
- Hand hygiene must be completed before and after waste transfer.
- Spill response kit must be available in every high-risk zone.

## Escalation
- Non-compliance events must be reported within the same shift.
- Repeat breach triggers infection-control committee review.

## Source Guidance
This dummy policy is used for compliance and infection-control questions, including audit preparedness.
